urdu transliteration text:
Ek satai hui bachi ko sataya na karoDo not torment an already distressed girlEnglish Transliteration
@HujjatELC

Ek satai hui bachi ko sataya na karoAe parindo dare zindaan se guzra na karo
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
Do not torment an already distressed girl
O birds, do not fly past the prison gate

Ever since she is unable to sleep on her father’s chest
I cannot bear to see her in agony any more
How can I ask her to not cry any more

urdu transliteration text:
Tum to azaad fazao ke musafir thehreshaam hote hi pohoch jaoge tum ghar apnedard kya samjhoge tum log jila watno kezakhm jo sookh gaye hai unhe taaza na karo
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
You are travellers of the free skies
At dusk you will safely reach your home
How can you understand the pain of exiled ones
Do not refresh the wounds that have dried

urdu transliteration text:
Asr e aashoor se rehti hai ye sehmi sehmiaaj tak bujh na saki tashna dahani iskikal thi shehzadi magar aaj hai ek faryadighumzada pehle se hai ghum me izafa na karo
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
Since the evening of Ashura she is very frightened
Even today her thirst has not been fully quenched
Yesterday she was a princess, today a prisoner
She is already sad, do not increase her sadness

urdu transliteration text:
Badi mushkil se sulaya hai tasalli dekarjane kab hoga mayassar ise yasrab ka safarhoga ehsaan tumhara ye dile muztar parisko ehsaas aseeri ka dilaya na karo
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
With great difficulty have I put her to sleep
I wonder when she will be able to travel to Madina
Please have mercy on her anxious heart
Remind her not that she is a captive

urdu transliteration text:
Qaid khane me thi shamshaad ye zainab ki fughabaap ki yaad me bechain sakina hai yahaniski ankho me hai pehle se hi ashre ka samaaur gardish me yatima ka sitara na karo
Zainab cries out in the dungeons, O Shamshad (poet)
Please don’t make things worse for her
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
Sakina remains distressed in memory of her father
The images of Ashura still revolve in her eyes
